title: Alcohol-related liver risk documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

investigations.text: Supports age, hepatitis-risk context, jaundice, abdominal pain, weight loss, pruritus, comorbidity, prescribed or non-prescribed drug history, travel or exposure context, and alcohol history.
investigations.rationale: The exact clinical-history section supports documenting alcohol history, associated liver symptoms, comorbidity, medicines, travel, exposure, weight loss, pruritus and abdominal pain without diagnosing alcohol-related liver disease.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports age, hepatitis-risk context, jaundice, abdominal pain, weight loss, pruritus, comorbidity, prescribed or non-prescribed drug history, travel or exposure context, and alcohol history.

source.title: Guidelines on the management of abnormal liver blood tests

exact_section.heading: Response to abnormal liver blood tests — clinical history

investigations.text: Requires review of previous results, past medical history, current condition, the specific abnormal analyte, and clinical context rather than inferring significance from magnitude alone.
investigations.rationale: The exact section supports review of prior and current laboratory context without interpreting significance solely from values.
investigations.evidence_extract: Requires review of previous results, past medical history, current condition, the specific abnormal analyte, and clinical context rather than inferring significance from magnitude alone.

source.title: Guidelines on the management of abnormal liver blood tests

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 2–3 — prior results and clinical context

investigations.text: Supports clinician-reviewed abdominal ultrasound and hepatitis B or C testing in the recommendation-qualified adult liver-aetiology-screen context; investigation choice remains clinician-led.
investigations.rationale: The exact section supports recording already available imaging or aetiology-screen results without ordering them.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed abdominal ultrasound and hepatitis B or C testing in the recommendation-qualified adult liver-aetiology-screen context; investigation choice remains clinician-led.

source.title: Guidelines on the management of abnormal liver blood tests

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 4–6 — liver aetiology screen
exact_section.locator: PDF pages 1 and 10; lines 68–86 and 899 onward

investigations.text: Supports clinician-led further investigation or referral based on synthetic failure, suspicious symptoms or signs, analyte pattern, persistence, risk factors, and clinical context without automatically assigning urgency.
investigations.rationale: The exact section supports clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, follow-up and questions without generating alcohol counseling, treatment, referral, or urgency.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led further investigation or referral based on synthetic failure, suspicious symptoms or signs, analyte pattern, persistence, risk factors, and clinical context without automatically assigning urgency.

source.title: Guidelines on the management of abnormal liver blood tests

exact_section.heading: Response pathway and Recommendation 10
exact_section.locator: PDF pages 2, 9, and 12; figure 1 and Recommendation 10

source_limitations: Amount and pattern, dependence, withdrawal risk, objective results, diagnosis, severity, safeguarding, advice, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
